sections.paragraph:
κόπος, ου, ὁ

① a state of discomfort or distress, trouble, difficulty, a transferred sense of κόπος=‘beating’ (s. κόπτω; Trag.; pap; Ps 106:12; Sir 22:13; 1 Macc 10:15; ParJer 5:6; Jos., Ant. 2, 257; Just., D. 68, 2) κόπους (κόπον) παρέχειν τινί (cause) trouble (for) someone, bother someone (κόπους παρέχειν τινί PTebt 21, 10 [115 b.c.]; BGU 844, 12; PGissUniv 27, 13f [IIIa.d.]; PGM 14b, 4f; κόπον παρ. τινί Sir 29:4) Mt 26:10; Mk 14:6; Lk 11:7; 18:5; Gal 6:17; Hv 3, 3, 2; AcPlCor 2:34. πολλοὺς κόπους ἠντληκώς after he had endured many hardships Hs 5, 6, 2a (cp. Did.. Gen. 105, 9). W. other terms relating to a peristasis (FDanker, 2 Cor [Augsburg Comm.], ’89, 85–91; 180–86) 2 Cor 6:5 and 11:23; on 10:15 s. 2 below.

② to engage in activity that is burdensome, work, labor, toil (Eur., Aristoph.; SIG 761, 6 [I b.c.]; PAmh 133, 11; POxy 1482, 6; LXX; En; TestIss 3:5; Apc4Esdr Fgm. a; ApcSed 14:2; Jos., Ant. 3, 25; 8, 244; Iren.1, 13, 5 [Harv I 122, 3]; Did., Gen. 104, 7.) sing. κ. τῆς ἀγάπης labor of love, i.e. loving service 1 Th 1:3. W. ἔργα Rv 2:2. W. ἱδρώς B 10:4. W. μόχθος (q.v.) 2 Cor 11:27; 1 Th 2:9; 2 Th 3:8; Hs 5, 6, 2b. ὁ κ. ὑμῶν οὐκ ἔστιν κενός your labor is not in vain 1 Cor 15:58. μήπως εἰς κενὸν γένηται ὁ κ. ἡμῶν that our work may not be fruitless 1 Th 3:5. Fig. of work at harvest time εἰς τὸν κ. τινὸς εἰσέρχεσθαι enter into someone’s labor i.e. reap the rewards of another person’s work J 4:38; τὸν μισθὸν λαμβάνειν κατὰ τὸν κ. receive pay in accordance w. the work done 1 Cor 3:8. ὅπου πλείων κ., πολὺ κέρδος the greater the toil, the richer the gain IPol 1:3.—Pl., of individual acts (En 7:3) 2 Cor 10:15 (6:5 and 11:23 appear to fit best under 1 above); Rv 14:13. Also abstr. for concr. reward for labor (Sir 14:15) Hm 2:4; Hs 9, 24, 2f.—AvHarnack, Κόπος (κοπιᾶν, οἱ κοπιῶντες) im frühchristl. Sprachgebr.: ZNW 27, 1928, 1–10; HKuist, Bibl. Review 16, ’31, 245–49. B. 540.—DELG s.v. κόπτω. M-M. TW. Spicq. Sv.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGissUniv PGissUniv = Mitteilungen aus der Papyrussammlung der Giessener Universitätsbibliothek—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

παρ.̠par. = parallel

Hv Hv = Visions

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Apc4Esdr Apc4Esdr = Apocalypse of Fourth Esdras—List 2

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

sing. sing. = singular

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

abstr. abstr. = abstractum pro concreto (abstract for the concrete)

concr. concr. = concrete(ly), cp. abstr.

Hm Hm = Mandates

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 558
